The plan of the specific sub-structure 1: {'name': '22×3 Deck with 18×2-Deep Twin Side-Truss Underframe', 'description': 'A long, 3-wide deck beam with twin side trusses beneath its edges. The top deck spans 22 units (so 1-unit overhang onto each terrain top), while the bottom longitudinal chords and truss webbing are restricted to the gap region only. The side trusses consist of:
        - Top chords: the outer rows of the deck.
        - Bottom chords: left and right longitudinal rows 2 units below the deck edges, each 18 units long (y from −9 to +9).
        - Vertical posts: at periodic y-positions, 2-block-tall posts connect the top and bottom chords.
        - Diagonal braces: alternate-direction Braces between posts along each side, forming triangulated bays to stiffen the span.
        Bottom transverse ties at the same y-positions couple both side trusses to resist torsion and keep the deck flat under central load.', 'components': '- Default base: 1× Stone starting block (1×1×1, mass 0.25) used as the central deck block.
        - Small Wooden Block (1×1×1, mass 0.3):
          • Top deck: 3 × 22 = 66 blocks (one of these positions is occupied by the Stone base).
          • Bottom chords: 2 × 18 = 36 blocks (left and right, y from −9 to +9).
          • Vertical posts: 6 y-positions per side × 2 sides × 2 blocks tall = 24 blocks.
          • Bottom transverse ties: 6 blocks (one at x=0 for each chosen y-position).
          Approximate total wooden blocks: 66 + 36 + 24 + 6 = 132 wooden blocks (plus 1 stone).
        - Brace (connector, mass 0.5):
          • Side truss diagonals: 5 bays per side × 1 brace per bay × 2 sides = 10 Braces.
          (Optional: add 2–4 more braces for extra torsional coupling if needed; omit for lower cost in the base design.)
        - No Powered Wheels, Torches, Water Cannons, or Winches are used.', 'assembly': 'Orientation and overall size:
        - Bridge long axis along y (north–south), width along x (east–west), depth along z (up–down).
        - Final deck planform: 22 (y) × 3 (x). Overall depth: about 4 units (1-unit deck thickness + 2-unit separation + 1-unit bottom chord thickness).

        Stepwise arrangement:
        1) Top deck (z top layer):
           - Start from the default Stone base as the central deck block at y≈0, x=0.
           - Build the center row along y to achieve a continuous 22-block row from y=−11 to y=+11.
           - Add left (x=−1) and right (x=+1) deck rows along the same y range to make a 3×22 deck.
        2) Bottom chords (z lower layer), within the gap only:
           - Left bottom chord: under the left deck row (x=−1), place a straight 18-block line from y=−9 to y=+9, 2 blocks below the top deck.
           - Right bottom chord: mirror under the right deck row (x=+1), same y range and depth.
           Important: keep all bottom chord blocks and any under-deck posts/diagonals within |y| ≤ 9 to avoid interference with the terrain’s vertical faces at y=±10.
        3) Vertical posts (connect top and bottom chords):
           - At y= −9, −5, −1, +1, +5, +9 (6 positions), at x=−1 and x=+1, stack two Small Wooden Blocks downward from the underside of the deck row so they reach and attach to the bottom chords. This yields 2-block-tall posts at each selected y.
        4) Bottom transverse ties:
           - At each of the same 6 y positions, place one Small Wooden Block at x=0 at the bottom-chord level to connect the inner faces of the left and right bottom chord blocks (the bottom chord blocks at x=±1 attach to the cross-tie’s ±x faces). These ties couple both side trusses and resist torsion.
        5) Diagonal Braces (triangulate the side trusses):
           - For each side (x=−1 and x=+1), between successive posts, install one Brace per bay in alternating directions (“N” pattern). For example, on the left side:
             • Bay (−9 to −5): Brace from the bottom chord at y=−9 up to the deck edge at y=−5.
             • Bay (−5 to −1): Brace from top at y=−5 down to bottom at y=−1.
             • Bay (−1 to +1): Brace from bottom at y=−1 up to top at y=+1.
             • Bay (+1 to +5): Brace from top at y=+1 down to bottom at y=+5.
             • Bay (+5 to +9): Brace from bottom at y=+5 up to top at y=+9.
           - Mirror the same alternating pattern on the right side.
           - Ensure each Brace connects between actual block faces (e.g., the underside of a top-deck edge block to the top face of the corresponding bottom-chord block in the next panel).
        6) Clearance check:
           - Confirm no bottom chord, post, or brace extends beyond |y|=9. The deck alone may extend to |y|=11 to sit on the terrain tops at y=±10 to ±∞. This prevents any under-deck element at z<5 from intersecting the terrain mass.', 'motion_control': 'None. The bridge is passive. After placement at (x=0, y=0, z=7), let it gently settle under gravity; its deck ends will rest on the terrain tops at z=5, with the underframe suspended within the gap.', 'function': '- The 3-wide deck provides sufficient platform area for the 2.5×2.5 cargo, ensuring it sits centered without tipping.
        - Twin side trusses (top edge rows as top chords, bottom edge rows as bottom chords) resist bending; diagonal Braces create triangular panels for stiffness, reducing midspan deflection under increasing cargo load.
        - Bottom transverse ties make the two side trusses act together and resist twisting when the load is not perfectly centered.
        - Deck overlength (22) ensures bearing onto both terrain tops, while the lower members are confined to |y| ≤ 9 to avoid terrain collision.', 'design_requirements': '- Deck must be exactly 22 units long (y from −11 to +11 relative to the central block) and 3 units wide (x=−1, 0, +1).
        - All under-deck members (bottom chords, posts, diagonal braces, and bottom ties) must lie within |y| ≤ 9 so that nothing at z < 5 intersects the terrain vertical faces at y=±10.
        - Vertical spacing between top deck and bottom chords should be 2 blocks to achieve a deep section (overall depth ≈ 4 blocks). Posts must be tall enough (2 blocks) to connect top and bottom chords.
        - Maintain continuous, face-to-face attachments between adjacent Small Wooden Blocks for structural integrity; Braces must connect between attachable faces of existing blocks only.
        - No powered components should be included to avoid unintended motion; the bridge must settle and remain static under load by gravity and friction.
        - Positions may be micro‑adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.'}

Orientation and scope
- Global axes: x east (+), y north (+), z up (+). Long bridge axis along y, width along x, depth along z.
- Build starts from the default 1×1×1 base block at deck center (x=0, y≈0).
- Position adjustment note: To satisfy the “exactly 22 units long” deck requirement, each deck row uses 22 blocks spanning y from −11 to +10 inclusive (the base occupies the x=0, y=0 center of the middle row). All under-deck members (bottom chords, posts, ties, diagonals) are confined to |y| ≤ 9. This keeps the intended geometry while maintaining the underframe clearance constraint. If a later review requires the deck endpoints at ±11, we can extend the deck uniformly to ±11 by adjusting the south end +1 (adds 3 deck blocks); functional intent remains unchanged.

Assembly format note (connectors and parallel rows)
- For clarity and to ensure correct attachment points on a multi-row structure, “relative position” entries reference the specific block ID and face used (e.g., attached to the north face of Block 1). This deviates slightly from “previous block” only in cases where the correct face belongs to a non-immediately previous block (typical for parallel rows and connectors). This preserves buildability and face-to-face attachment integrity.

Notes and checks
- Structural continuity: All Small Wooden Blocks are face-to-face attached along rows, posts, chords, and ties, creating continuous load paths. Braces connect between actual block faces, as required.
- Underframe clearance: All under-deck members (posts, bottom chords, ties, braces) are confined within |y| ≤ 9.
- Depth: Bottom chords are 2 blocks below the deck; 2-block posts connect deck edges to bottom chords, yielding an overall section depth ≈ 4 blocks.
- No powered components used; passive structure settles under gravity.
- If any overlap or face occupation conflicts are detected during actual placement (e.g., local terrain contact at end blocks), position adjustment note: slide the entire structure +1 on y if needed, or trim/extend deck ends symmetrically by ±1 to maintain 22-block total while keeping underframe within ±9; functional intent unchanged.

Step 0 – Coordinate and block model used for checking
- Base block: default 1×1×1 cubic starting block. I set its center at (x=0, y=0, z=0). All attachments are face-to-face, so the centers of neighboring 1×1×1 blocks differ by exactly 1 unit along the axis of attachment.
- Small Wooden Block: 1×1×1. Minimum non-overlap center-to-center separation equals 1 unit along at least one axis (0.5 + 0.5).
- Brace: connector, no volume, no collider. Only validation is whether it connects two actual block faces; no collision checks needed for the brace bodies.

Step 1 – Deck rows: positions and collision checks
Deck center row (x=0), 22-long from y=−11..+10 inclusive; all at z=0:
- Base (Stone): (0, 0, 0) given
- Blocks:
  • ID 1..10: (0, 1..10, 0)
  • ID 11..20: (0, −1..−10, 0)
  • ID 21: (0, −11, 0)
Collision checks (pattern, verified on multiple entries):
- Each deck center-row block has neighbors:
  • To the south/north along y by 1 unit (e.g., ID 1 at (0,1,0) vs Base at (0,0,0): Δy=1). This equals the minimum safe separation 1; face-to-face; no overlap.
  • Across x to left/right deck blocks at same y (e.g., ID 1 vs ID 23 at (−1,1,0) and ID 45 at (1,1,0): Δx=1). Also equals minimum safe separation; no overlap.
- Result: All center-row deck placements are collision-free and face-to-face continuous.

- Successive left-row blocks along y: Δy=1; OK.
- Left-row to center-row at same y: Δx=1; OK.
- Left-row to right-row at same y: Δx=2; OK (non-touching, no overlap).

Collision checks mirror the left row; all face-to-face or separated by at least 2 units; no overlaps.

Step 2 – Vertical posts: positions and collision checks

  • ID 74: (−1, 5, −1), ID 75: (−1, 5, −2)
  • ID 76: (−1, 9, −1), ID 77: (−1, 9, −2)
